channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Trump’s Religious Liberty Commission takes aim at separation of church and state
item.description: The Trump administration issued a draft report from the commission that says the separation concept is a legal error and that Americans should view religion as an “essential support.” The 224-page report also recommended the DOJ issue guidance to promote “an originalist understanding” of how the Constitution sees the relationship between religion and government.

item.title: The Great American State Fair feels rushed, simulated and oddly sterile, our columnist Philip Kennicott writes
item.description: The Trump administration’s signature 250th celebration on the National Mall lacks nostalgia and feels more like a trade show for Christian groups, tourist boards and the military industrial complex.

item.title: Appeals court rules against Trump administration on air pollution limits
item.description: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit ruled that air pollution limits tightened by the Environmental Protection Agency under President Joe Biden should remain in force, thwarting efforts by the Trump administration, the U.S. Chamber of Commerce and other groups to vacate that standard and return to a previous one.

item.title: U.S. strikes Iranian military sites after ship was hit in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. targeted Iranian missile and drone storage locations Friday in what Central Command called a “powerful response” to “unwarranted aggression.” President Donald Trump accused Iran of violating a ceasefire that was solidified in a memorandum of understanding signed by the two countries last week.

item.title: The Supreme Court errs in this immigration ruling, George F. Will writes
item.description: The justices get it wrong on temporary protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants.

item.title: Ukrainian drones drive Russia to declare emergency in occupied Crimea
item.description: Weeks of punishing strikes have disrupted water, fuel and electricity supplies across the peninsula, which Ukraine’s president has vowed to reclaim.

item.title: Trump administration will decide who gets to use latest upgrade to ChatGPT, a major expansion of its regulation of Silicon Valley
item.description: The administration came to power preaching a laissez-faire approach to AI but has lately increased oversight of the industry.

item.title: Lewis George racks up most support in a D.C. mayoral primary in a generation, spanning race, class and geography
item.description: D.C.’s presumptive next mayor, a democratic socialist, has amassed more than 75,000 votes in the most dominant primary performance since Adrian Fenty won every precinct in 2006.

item.title: Carolyn Hax chat: My sister shut me out because of her religion. Do I attend her wedding?
item.description: Carolyn Hax chat: My sister shut me out because of her religion. Do I attend her wedding?

item.title: Former Trump adviser John Bolton pleads guilty in classified files case
item.description: Bolton pleaded guilty in federal court Friday. He served as national security adviser in Donald Trump’s first administration before becoming a vocal critic of the president.

item.title: I’m an evangelical. Here’s the real problem with the tradwife trend.
item.description: When it comes to marriage and motherhood, traditional and biblical are not the same.

item.title: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record.
item.description: Take today’s news quiz and complete your point total for the week.

item.title: Nursing homes, factories brace for fallout from Supreme Court ruling that could affect more than 1 million immigrants
item.description: The high court’s decision to strip Haitians and Syrians of their protections from deportation had immediate ripple effects for immigrants and their employers.

item.title: Supreme Court rulings. Iran attack. Venezuela rescuers. NYC rent freeze. RFK Jr. Europe heat deaths. Anti-vaxxers. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: California billionaire tax is headed for the ballot despite opposition from top Democrats
item.description: The push to put a billionaire tax to a popular vote in blue California has exposed deep divides on the left, even as Democratic politicians across the country rally around calls for the wealthy to pay more. The 5 percent tax would raise tens of billions for health care, but the issue is pitting the populist mood of the public against many Democrats’ fears that the measure will push the ultrawealthy to join a broader exodus from the state.

item.title: New York City passes rent freeze, delivering on a key piece of Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s agenda
item.description: After months of hearings that included public and expert testimony, the Rent Guidelines Board handed down its decision, which caps rent for up to two years on about 40 percent of the New York’s housing stock — generally apartment buildings that were constructed before 1974. Landlords and real estate representatives panned the decision, saying it could devastate already-meager profits and drive bankruptcy.

item.title: What an expert in plant-based food eats for protein, calcium and more
item.description: We wanted to learn what a registered dietitian who follows a strictly plant-based diet eats in a typical day. So, we caught up with Nichole Dandrea-Russert to find out what her meals are like, why she avoids using the term “vegan” and why she recommends eating dark chocolate on a regular basis.

item.title: King Charles reveals his personal tax bill, in a first for a British monarch. Spoiler alert: He’s very rich.
item.description: The disclosure was partly intended to contain the damage from revelations about the finances of the king’s disgraced brother, Andrew.

item.title: A punching bag. ‘A graveyard.’ A new temptation for Trump.
item.description: A punching bag. ‘A graveyard.’ A new temptation for Trump.

item.title: Meet the megadonors pouring more than $1.3 billion into the 2026 election
item.description: Billionaires and newly minted trillionaire Elon Musk make up the top individual donors.

item.title: Ship attacked in Strait of Hormuz as Iran threatens U.N.-backed route
item.description: The attack on a cargo ship comes after a spike in vessels using a new shipping lane set up by the U.N. and Oman.

item.title: Scientists have just unlocked the secrets of an ancient scroll torched by Mount Vesuvius
item.description: After a centuries-long struggle, scholars managed to read five feet of text, using machine-learning methods they hope can unlock further discoveries.

item.title: RFK Jr. urged Iowa candidate to make deal to help GOP win House seat, per audio recording
item.description: The health secretary urged Iowa Libertarian candidates to drop out of key races to help Republicans keep control of Congress, according to interviews and a recording obtained by The Post. The effort highlights mounting GOP anxiety over the battle for the House.

item.title: Federal court blocks Trump’s executive order limiting mail ballots
item.description: The decision comes a month after another judge allowed Trump’s order to stand. The latest ruling sidelines major provisions of the order despite the earlier ruling.

item.title: Supreme Court lets Trump end temporary protections for migrants
item.description: The decision could allow the government to deport hundreds of thousands of people starting this year.

item.title: Supreme Court rules that migrants are not entitled to apply for asylum while standing on Mexico side of border
item.description: A group of migrants sued to block the policy, which began informally under the Obama administration, arguing that they should be entitled to seek asylum even if they were stopped before entering the United States.

item.title: Supreme Court strikes down Hawaii limits on carrying guns in public
item.description: The decision is the latest by the conservative majority to loosen firearms restrictions in recent terms.

item.title: Supreme Court blocks thousands of lawsuits claiming Monsanto's weed killer Roundup causes cancer
item.description: On the line are billions of dollars, the fate of tens of thousands of lawsuits filed by cancer victims and the future of an herbicide farmers say is crucial to the nation’s food supply but health groups warn is a danger.

item.title: We’re dermatologists. Sunscreen isn’t the problem.
item.description: Every year, a safe-sunscreen guide sparks needless concern.

item.title: A big night for this person in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Thursday points.

item.title: Venezuela earthquakes. Iran vote. Pete Hegseth. Election megadonors. Park deaths. Moving south. World Cup heroes. Catch up now.
item.description: The 7.

item.title: At least 164 dead as powerful earthquakes hit Venezuela
item.description: The back-to-back quakes toppled buildings in the capital and were among Venezuela’s strongest in a century. The death toll is expected to rise.

item.title: Under pressure from Trump, Senate reverses course on resolution to end war in Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump had complained that the passage of a similar resolution Tuesday undermined ongoing negotiations with Tehran over its nuclear program. The resolution Wednesday failed 50-47 on a late-night procedural vote after two Republicans changed their votes.

item.title: Meet the wet-nosed, treat-loving pups who guard against smuggled goods
item.description: Recruits for CBP's Beagle Brigade are trained at the National Detector Dog Training Center in Georgia, where they learn to detect prohibited agricultural items at U.S. ports of entry. The dogs, motivated by treats, undergo rigorous training to identify scents such as citrus and pork.

item.title: Internal memo orders staff not to reveal deaths in national parks
item.description: Despite a string of recent deaths at national parks, internal guidance prohibits park staff or other Interior Department employees from directly notifying the public about such incidents. A memo issued in December and reviewed by The Washington Post states that Interior employees, including those who communicate with the media, are no longer permitted to confirm deaths or details about severe injuries, a restriction that current and former rangers say breaks with the department’s previous disclosure policy.

item.title: Trump vents frustration with Republicans at contentious lunch, his first meeting with GOP senators in months
item.description: The meeting came after the president repeatedly clashed with Republicans in recent days, including his decision hours before the lunch started not to sign a bipartisan housing bill Congress passed with overwhelming support until lawmakers pass voting legislation that Republicans repeatedly have said does not have enough votes.

item.title: $0 co-pays could save billions
item.description: $0 co-pays could save billions

item.title: Hegseth thwarted internal efforts to extend influential Army general’s career
item.description: Gen. Christopher Donahue, seen as a top warfighter, is the latest apparent casualty in a purge of senior military leaders by the Trump administration.

item.title: Are ChatGPT and other AI chatbots biased toward the left or right? We tested them.
item.description: The Post tested ChatGPT, Gemini and other chatbots with political questions, and the results suggest that the AI tools have different political leanings.

item.title: Rocked by the Iran war, the United Arab Emirates sours on Trump: ‘We got played’
item.description: Across the Persian Gulf region, the president was viewed as a pro-business ally, but his decision to wage war on Iran and his erratic conduct have tarnished his image.

item.title: A restaurant was out of the dish I wanted. Should they give me a free drink? Our food critic is taking your questions now.
item.description: A restaurant was out of the dish I wanted. Should they give me a free drink? Our food critic is taking your questions now.

item.title: Trump abruptly cancels signing of bipartisan bill on affordable housing
item.description: The president posted on social media that the signing was off until the Senate passes his election legislation. It is unclear whether he intends to kill the housing bill.

item.title: Trump is ‘draining the swamp.’ Just not the one he intended.
item.description: The contractor in chief turned the National Mall into a costly mess.

item.title: Why Trump’s algae problem is much bigger than the Reflecting Pool
item.description: In his battle to clear algae from the water by the Lincoln Memorial, the president has overlooked the real causes of unsightly — and often dangerous — algal blooms.

item.title: A resignation in today’s On the Record
item.description: A resignation in today’s On the Record

item.title: What Tuesday’s primaries say about the future of the Democratic Party
item.description: Democratic socialist candidates won key races in New York, while more establishment candidates prevailed in other contests.

item.title: Primary results. War powers. Reflecting Pool. ICE protesters. AI bias test. College credits. Perfect bedtime. Catch up now.
item.description: The 7.

item.title: Rep. April McClain Delaney (D-Maryland) fends off wine mogul David Trone’s comeback bid after a bitter primary battle with millions spent
item.description: The acrimonious campaign marked a comeback bid for Trone, who represented Maryland’s western-most congressional district for six years before losing his $60 million self-financed bid for the Senate two years ago.

item.title: All 3 Mamdani-backed candidates win U.S. House primaries in New York City. An earlier alert misstated how many incumbents had lost.
item.description: Brad Lander defeated two-term incumbent Rep. Dan Goldman in New York’s 10th District, while Claire Valdez won the Democratic primary to succeed Rep. Nydia Velázquez in the 7th District. In the 13th District, Darializa Avila Chevalier, a doctoral student and Mamdani campaign organizer, unseated Rep. Adriano Espaillat.

item.title: Dan Cox wins primary and will challenge Maryland Gov. Wes Moore. The MAGA-aligned Republican lost soundly to Moore in 2022.
item.description: Moore, running for a second term, is a rising star in the Democratic Party and a possible 2028 presidential contender.
